_note: Round-11 follow-up #3: a top-level UNTYPED dispatcher shadowed by a nested TYPED dispatcher with the same name. Pre-fix native computed `untyped_locals` as `all_locals \ typed_locals` BY NAME — the two `dispatch` bindings cancelled out, and the top-level `dispatch('bar')` call's name was never collected. Upstream's per-call check (`ComponentEvents.ts:256`) tests `eventDispatchers.some(d => !d.typing && d.name === call.name)`: at least ONE untyped dispatcher with the matching name. The shadow case has one (the top-level binding), so calls still collect. Native now uses a new `find_untyped_dispatcher_local_names` helper that counts typed-vs-total per name and keeps any name where the untyped count exceeds zero. Inner here exposes `bar` from the top-level untyped dispatcher's call AND `scoped` from the nested typed dispatcher; Consumer's `<Inner on:bar={handle}>` typechecks. Pre-fix `bar` would have been dropped from the events surface.
